# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'  // Import the necessary modules to handle requests and responses with NodeJS (Server Side)
// import your jwt library for decoding tokens in cookies if needed  
const YOUR_SECRET = "your-secret"    // Define a secret key that will be used when encoding or verifying JWTs 
                                    // This should not remain the same across different requests, it's recommended to use environment variables.
import { verify } from 'jsonwebtoken';   // Import jwt library for decoding tokens in cookies if needed (Server Side)   
const YOUR_TOKEN = "your-token"      // Define a token that will be used when encoding or verifying JWTs 
                                    // This should not remain the same across different requests, it's recommended to use environment variables.  
// Import your user model if needed (Server Side)   
import { User } from '../models/user';    
const YOUR_MODEL = new User();      // Define a instance of YourUserModel 
                                    // This should be replaced with the actual import statement for you Model in Next.js  
// Import your database if needed (Server Side)   
import { dbConnect } from '../config/db'     ;       // Replace this line by an appropriate DB connection function call based on where and how data is stored 
const YOUR_DB = "your-database"      // Define a name for the Database instance  
// Import your user model if needed (Server Side)   
import { User } from '../models/user';    
let db;       // Declare database connection variable as undefined at first. This will be set once we connect to DB 
export async function init() : Promise<void>{   // Define a method that gets called when the server starts (Server Side)   
db = await dbConnect(YOUR_DB);      // Connect with your Database and assign it into database variable if connection is successful. If not, throw an error 
}    
export async function verifyRequest(request: NextRequest): Promise<User | null> {   // Define a method that verifies the request (Server Side)   
let token;      // Declare JWT as undefined at first and assign it into variable if cookie exists. If not, return an error 
if ('cookie' in request.headers && 'authorization' in request.headers['cookie']) {   // Check for cookies existence (Server Side)   
token = parseCookies(request).Token;      // Parse the token from your JWT cookie and assign it into variable if exists, else return an error 
}else{     // If no tokens found in headers then exit this block. This is to prevent any errors when there's a lack of authentication data (Server Side)   
return null;   }      // Return the user as undefined at first and assign it into variable if token not exists, else return an error 
let decoded: User | null = null ;     // Declare Decode JWT result will be stored here. This is to store information about a validated jwt (Server Side)   
try {      // Try block for handling exceptions in case of any errors occur during the process above  
decoded =  verify(token, YOUR_SECRET);     // Verify your JWT token and assign it into variable if verified successfully. If not then return an error 
} catch (error) {    // Catch block for handling exceptions in case of any errors occur during the process above  
return null;      }       // Return user as undefined at first, else throw exception or handle this scenario according to your application's requirement and logic. This is a common practice when dealing with JWT verification (Server Side)   
if(decoded){     // If decode successfully then only proceed further  
return decoded;  }      // Return the user if token verified, else return an error or handle this scenario according to your application's requirement and logic. This is a common practice when dealing with JWT verification (Server Side)   
}else{     // If not successfully verify then only proceed further  
return null;  }      // Return the user as undefined at first, else throw exception or handle this scenario according to your application's requirement and logic. This is a common practice when dealing with JWT verification (Server Side)   
} ) ;     };       // End of Async function for verifying request
